windows.embed_text_v2: pouring molten golden wax from a grey metal pitcher into amber glass candle jars with centered wicks held by yellow wick bars, filling a row of jars on Avery label sheets, top-down
windows.summary_v2: Molten golden wax is poured from a metal pitcher into a row of wicked amber glass jars on label sheets, filling each candle around its centered wick.

seconds_v2.desc: Top-down view of molten golden wax being poured from a grey pitcher into the first amber glass jar in a row of wicked jars on Avery label sheets.
The honey-colored wax streams into the first amber jar, its centered wick held by a yellow wick bar; neighboring jars already partly filled.
A thin stream of molten wax continues filling the left jar around its wick, the pour pitcher tilted at bottom of frame.
The pitcher shifts as golden wax fills the jar near the wick sticker, more amber jars lined up to the right.
Wax level rises in the jar; the steady stream pours beside the centered wick as the row of jars fills.
Molten wax keeps pouring into the amber jar around the yellow-tabbed wick, surface glossy gold.
The pour continues into the next jar, golden wax swirling around the upright wick, jars seen from above.
The silver pouring pitcher tilts over the filled amber jars, finishing pouring molten wax around the centered wicks.
